raw_consumer_statement:
So I filed a dispute with my credit card company based on a purchase I made from a merchant. 
On XX/XX/year>, XXXX, I emailed Citi 's disputes department requesting ( Under the Fair Credit Billing Act FCBA ), formally requesting a copy of all " compelling evidence '' submitted by the merchant in order to win the dispute. 

Based on a phone call with citi 's dispute department ( Reference # XXXX ) on XX/XX/XXXX, they said they would send me the evidence, but it would take XXXX hours. 
However, after XXXX hours passed, I did not receive the evidence - therefore, city failed to comply with the procedural requirements under the law of the XXXX. 

I have attached a PDF file which is correspondence I have had with the merchant trying to resolve the issue and it clearly indicates and displays the lack of involvement from the merchant. They essentially ignored my follow up emails asking for them to provide me with an update. 
I have also attached a screenshot of the email I sent to city cards dispute, and Billings department with time stamps requesting the information that I am required by law under the XXXX. 

Under XXXX CFR XXXX ( f ), Pursuant to XXXX XXXX. XXXX ( XXXX ), a creditor who fails to comply with the procedural requirements of the XXXX forfeits its right to collect the disputed amount and any related finance charges, regardless of whether a billing error actually occurred. 
Furthermore, please be advised that under XXXX XXXX. XXXX, a creditor who fails to comply with any requirement of the XXXX is liable for actual damages, twice the amount of any finance charge ( up to {$5000.00} ), and reasonable attorney 's fees.
